channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 2 people are dead in a small plane collision at a southern Arizona airport
item.description: A midair collision involving two small planes in southern Arizona killed two people Wednesday morning, authorities said. Federal air-safety investigators said each plane had two people aboard when they collided at Marana Regional Airport on the outskirts of Tucson.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order targeting any federal benefits going to those in the country illegally
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed an executive order intended to identify and eliminate any federal financial benefits going to immigrants who entered the country illegally, something already prohibited existing the law.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: NASA looking for ways to destroy asteroid that could strike Earth, kill city
item.description: NASA and other space agencies are attempting to come up with a way to destroy a massive asteroid unlikely to strike Earth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 things to watch at this year's CPAC
item.description: Conservatives are gathering in National Harbor, Md., this week for the annual Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in what is poised to be a celebration for the political right following President Trump’s election victory in November.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: This rookie QB card could be worth $50K if you pull it from a new Topps set
item.description: Denver Broncos rookie quarterback Bo Nix impressed on the field, and now a Colorado sports card store is ready to pay $50,000 to keep a unique card featuring him close to home.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KREX 5

item.title: DOGE notches courtroom wins as Elon Musk crusades to slash federal government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Although some parts of President Donald Trump ’s agenda are getting bogged down by litigation, Elon Musk ’s Department of Government Efficiency is having better luck in the courtroom.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Feds pause contract providing legal services to thousands of unaccompanied migrant children
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – An El Paso nonprofit plans to sue the federal government for halting their ability to provide legal services to migrant minors in federal custody.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Measles cases are rising: What to know
item.description: A measles outbreak is happening in Texas, where there are 58 confirmed cases, and there have been confirmed cases in New Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: New York rep. says Trump has committed 'dozens of impeachable offenses' so far
item.description: “Donald Trump has, in his first month, probably committed dozens of impeachable offenses,” Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) said in a recent interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Former NFL player arrested while protesting at California city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a Huntington Beach City Council meeting on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the town's Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: The white man who pleaded guilty to shooting a Black teen who rang a wrong doorbell dies
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — An 86-year-old Missouri man has died just days after pleading guilty to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, prosecutors announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New, cheaper iPhone 16e has 'best battery life ever,' Apple says
item.description: "iPhone 16e has the best battery life ever on a 6.1-inch iPhone, lasting up to six hours longer than iPhone 11 and up to 12 hours longer than all generations of iPhone SE," Apple said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Polar vortex makes much of US colder than Greenland, but warmth is coming. Then more cold
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The polar vortex hit its peak across much of America on Wednesday, with an icy grip that made Arctic Greenland seem like a toasty vacation spot in comparison. Even Mars has been warmer than North Dakota this week. But there's hope.
item.dc:creator: BY SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who admitted to shooting Black teen at wrong door dies before sentencing
item.description: Andrew Lester, a man who pleaded guilty last week to second-degree assault after he shot Ralph Yarl, an unarmed teenage boy in Kansas City in 2023, has died, according to a Clay County prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Refunds delayed for taxpayers claiming Earned Income Tax Credit
item.description: The EITC helps low-to-moderate income workers and families get a tax break to reduce the taxes they owe or possibly receive a larger refund.
item.dc:creator: Brian R Svendsen, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's firing of 1,000 national park workers raises concerns about maintenance and operating hours
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has fired about 1,000 newly hired National Park Service employees who maintain and clean parks, educate visitors and perform other functions as part of its broad-based effort to downsize government.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Video: Woman pulled from tight storm drain in California
item.description: The woman, who was "found unclothed above the waist," was taken to a local hospital for evaluation, though she "had no obvious injuries.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: Kamala Harris signs with major Hollywood talent agency
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris has signed with the Creative Artists Agency, one of Hollywood's top talent and sports agencies. In a post on Instagram, CAA revealed signed with the agency "for representation in all areas, with a focus on speaking engagements and publishing.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: DOGE affiliate may access IRS taxpayer records: Report
item.description: An IRS employee with affiliations to the Department of Government Efficiency may be able to gain access to IRS taxpayer records.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Video: Vehicle launches off overpass, plummets onto freeway below
item.description: Dash camera video captured the horrifying moment the car veers off the overpass, falling onto Interstate 5, and spinning on the nose of the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Aircrafts collide in midair near Arizona airport; 2 reported dead
item.description: The NTSB said one of the airplanes landed "uneventfully" after the collision, while the other crashed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to multi-state killings held without bail
item.description: The Zizians have been tied to the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David Maland near the Canadian border in January and five other homicides in Vermont, Pennsylvania and California.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspects charged in connection to burglaries of several pro athletes
item.description: Seven Chilean nationals face federal charges related to the burglaries of several high profile athletes' homes.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KRON4

item.title: A comprehensive look at DOGE's firings and layoffs so far
item.description: Thousands of federal government employees have been shown the door in the first month of President Donald Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: A new article published this week in The Lancet medical journal found that more than half of the world's population lacks access to safe and affordable medical oxygen services, and most people outside of high-income countries go without adequate oxygen services for their medical conditions.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: North Carolina high school football players killed in ATV crash after house party; 3 adults charged
item.description: Three North Carolina adults have been arrested and charged in connection with a fatal August ATV crash that left two Rosman High School students dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-Israel protest in NYC devolves into violence and mayhem
item.description: A group of anti-Israel demonstrators descended on an Orthodox Jewish neighborhood in Brooklyn on Tuesday leading to violent clashes and chaotic scenes, according to reports and video.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Five pressing questions as RFK Jr. takes HHS reins
item.description: His entire Make America Healthy Again movement is based largely on undermining the scientific and public health messaging from the federal government, along with overhauling the status quo in the food and drug industries.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Police arrest apparent leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to multiple killings in the US
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The apparent leader of a cultlike group known as the Zizians has been arrested in Maryland along with another member of the group, Maryland State Police said Monday. Jack Lasota, 34, was arrested Sunday along with Michelle Zajko, 33, of Media, Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Anger, chaos and confusion take hold as federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Workers across the country responded with anger and confusion Friday as they grappled with the Trump administration 's aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce by ordering agencies to lay off probationary employees who have yet to qualify for civil service protec
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, BRIAN WITTE, MIKE HOUSEHOLDER and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4
